[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [40°49′12″N 73°35′49″W﻿ / ﻿40.8201°N 73.5969°W﻿ / 40.8201; -73.5969](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=LIU_Post&params=40.8201_N_73.5969_W_source:kolossus-dewiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Private university in Brookville, New York, US

This article is about LIU Post. For the main campus, see [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University").

Not to be confused with [Post University](/wiki/Post_University "Post University").

LIU Post

**LIU Post**, formally the **C.W. Post Campus of Long Island University** and often referred to as **C.W. Post**, is a [private university](/wiki/Private_university "Private university") in [Brookville, New York](/wiki/Brookville,_New_York "Brookville, New York"), United States. Located on [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), it is part of [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University") (LIU), and the largest school in the LIU system.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The campus is named after [C.W. Post](/wiki/C.W._Post "C.W. Post"), father of [Marjorie Merriweather Post](/wiki/Marjorie_Merriweather_Post "Marjorie Merriweather Post"), who sold her Long Island estate known as Hillwood to Long Island University in 1951 for $200,000 ($2,480,769 today).[[1]](#cite_note-LIUTakesEstate-1) Three years after acquiring the property, LIU renamed it C.W. Post College in honor of Post's father.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Campus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Campus")]

LIU Post is located on 307 acres (1.24 km2) of rolling hills in [Brookville, New York](/wiki/Brookville,_New_York "Brookville, New York"), on Long Island's [North Shore](/wiki/North_Shore_(Long_Island) "North Shore (Long Island)"). The area is sometimes datelined as [Greenvale](/wiki/Greenvale,_New_York "Greenvale, New York"), because there is no "Brookville" post office, and the school is in the zip code that is served by the Greenvale post office, which is to the west. [Greenvale station](/wiki/Greenvale_(LIRR_station) "Greenvale (LIRR station)") is the nearest [Long Island Rail Road](/wiki/Long_Island_Rail_Road "Long Island Rail Road") station.

Humanities Hall and Life Sciences/Pell Hall are the main educational buildings on campus, and house most of the core curriculum classes. Classes are also held in Hoxie Hall, Roth Hall, Lorber Hall, the Theater Film and Dance building, Sculpture Studio, Crafts Center, Fine Arts Center, B. Davis Schwartz Memorial Library, and the Kahn Discovery Center.

The [Tilles Center for the Performing Arts](/wiki/Tilles_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts "Tilles Center for the Performing Arts") is on the west side of the campus. Previously known as the Bush-Brown Concert Theater (named for the longtime Long Island University chancellor Dr. Albert Bush-Brown), the Tilles Center has hosted many musical and theatrical events.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The Hillwood Commons serves as the student activities center, and also houses several administrative offices, including financial aid and bursars office, as well as the Promise office which handles all student day-to-day activities (classes, student organizations, housing, etc).[[4]](#cite_note-4) Hillwood has a study lounge, commuter lounge, recreation lounge, and TV lounge (located on opposite sides of the two-story building) that are open as long as Hillwood is open. The Hillwood Cafe, Subway, and Starbucks are all located here and serve as the main dining areas, along with the Winnick Student Center serving as the single dining hall on campus for residential students.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The Hillwood Commons serves as a meeting area for resident and commuter students to get to know each other through informal association outside of the classroom. The Hillwood Commons area also houses the Campus Concierge, Hillwood Computer Lab, Hillwood Cinema, School Bookstore, and multiple student run businesses, such as Browse (electronics store), The Student Body Collective (Clothing boutique), and Sharknation (merchandise shop).[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The university's C.W. Post Interfaith Chapel is home of the Interfaith Center, which provides both religious services as well as partnerships with community organizations.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) The chapel was first conceived in 1968 by Bradley Delehanty and completed by the noted Long Island architectural firm Alfred Shaknis and Peter S. van Bloem in the classic Jeffersonian style [Georgian architecture](/wiki/Georgian_architecture "Georgian architecture") design as a tribute to all religious faiths. Included among its notable architectural features are a domed rotunda at the main sanctuary, as well as soaring [Doric](/wiki/Doric_Order "Doric Order") columns at the main entrance which call to mind the ancient Roman [Pantheon](/wiki/Pantheon,_Rome "Pantheon, Rome").[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Academics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Academics")]

| [Academic rankings](/wiki/College_and_university_rankings_in_the_United_States "College and university rankings in the United States") | |
| --- | --- |
| National | |
| [*Forbes*](/wiki/America%27s_Top_Colleges "America's Top Colleges")[[11]](#cite_note-Rankings_Forbes_NU-11) | 370 |
| [*U.S. News & World Report*](/wiki/U.S._News_%26_World_Report_Best_Colleges_Ranking "U.S. News & World Report Best Colleges Ranking")[[12]](#cite_note-Rankings_USNWR_NU-12) | 369 |

LIU Post offers undergraduate and graduate programs in the following colleges and schools:

* College of Communications, Art, and Design
* College of Liberal Arts and Sciences
* College of Management
* College of Education, Information and Technology
* School of Health Professions and Nursing
* Lewyt College of Veterinary Medicine

## Student life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Student life")]

LIU Post is located about 25 miles (40 km) east from [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). Students at the university predominantly come from eastern [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), and the [New York metropolitan area](/wiki/New_York_metropolitan_area "New York metropolitan area"), and there are a smaller number of students from elsewhere in the nation and from foreign nations. The university has eight Greek Life organizations on campus.

## Athletics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Athletics")]

Main article: [LIU Sharks](/wiki/LIU_Sharks "LIU Sharks")

Long Island University's athletic teams are known as the [LIU Sharks](/wiki/LIU_Sharks "LIU Sharks"). They compete in the [National Collegiate Athletic Association](/wiki/National_Collegiate_Athletic_Association "National Collegiate Athletic Association") at the [Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") level, the highest level of collegiate athletics. Prior to 2019, the university's athletic programs competed at the [Division II](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II "NCAA Division II") level. The programs are governed by the NCAA, the [Eastern College Athletic Conference](/wiki/Eastern_College_Athletic_Conference "Eastern College Athletic Conference") (ECAC), the [East Coast Conference](/wiki/East_Coast_Conference "East Coast Conference") (ECC), and the [Northeast-10 Conference](/wiki/Northeast-10_Conference "Northeast-10 Conference") (NE-10). Prior to 2019, the two LIU campuses had two athletics teams, C.W. Post had the [LIU Post Pioneers](/wiki/LIU_Post_Pioneers "LIU Post Pioneers"), and competed in Division II. The [LIU campus](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University") in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") competed at the [Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") level as the [Blackbirds](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds "LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds").

In July 2019, the two campuses merged their two athletics teams into a single unit competing in Division I, and assumed the name LIU Sharks.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In addition to its NCAA-sanctioned athletic programs, students may participate in various sports, including basketball, racquetball, swimming, and volleyball, for leisure at the Pratt Recreation Center. There is a fitness center for aerobic and cardiovascular workouts on the campus. The athletic fields and courts are used for recreational baseball, football, soccer, softball, and tennis.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Notable faculty

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notable faculty")]

* [T. K. Blue](/wiki/T._K._Blue "T. K. Blue"), leader of the jazz band
* [Bob Brier](/wiki/Bob_Brier "Bob Brier"), [Egyptologist](/wiki/Egyptology "Egyptology") and [mummy](/wiki/Mummy "Mummy") specialist
* [Paul Kim](/wiki/Paul_Kim_(pianist) "Paul Kim (pianist)"), musician

## External links

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: External links")]
